diagrams.title: Linked Stack Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the node structure and how nodes are connected in a linked stack
elements: TOP pointer pointing to first node
Node boxes with DATA and NEXT fields
Arrows showing link from each node to next
NULL at the bottom indicating end of stack
Visual comparison: empty stack (TOP=NULL) vs with elements
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how linked stack organizes nodes and the role of TOP pointer

diagrams.title: Linked Queue Structure
diagrams.description: Depicts the FRONT and REAR pointers in a linked queue with multiple elements
elements: FRONT pointer pointing to first node (where deletion occurs)
REAR pointer pointing to last node (where insertion occurs)
Node boxes showing DATA and NEXT fields
NULL at the end of queue
Shows both pointers pointing to same node when queue has one element
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how both ends are managed in a linked queue for efficient operations
